corpus.label: DH/OpenKit official corpus
corpus.limitation: Current official corpus is smaller than the 3,000-file target recorded in the approved solution; hydrate measurements remain official-corpus evidence, not large-corpus proof.

notes: Hydrate benchmark runs a normal Rust index preparation and then repeats HydratedGraphProjection::hydrate five times against the produced index state.
Index benchmark artifacts now also expose single-run link_ms and graph_hydration_ms from dh-indexer::IndexReport.

hydrateGraph.freshnessReason: 2 indexed file(s) have degraded partial freshness

deletionGateDecision.reason: RGA-07E resolves the missing hydrate-p95 instrumentation gap for Rust benchmark artifacts, but the captured measurement is degraded/official-corpus-only and does not address official parity (RGA-07G) or incremental performance failures (RGA-07F).

limitations: Benchmark was run in debug profile on a local working tree; it is not a release-profile SLA.
Hydrate freshness was partial because two indexed files had degraded partial freshness and the index report had 32 not_current files.
The corpus is below the 3,000-file target, so the <=2s hydrate p95 threshold is measured only on the official DH/OpenKit corpus, not on a 3k-file corpus.
RGA-07E did not optimize incremental performance and did not produce final official parity.
